content.full_text:
CITY OF STOCKTON
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
425 North El Dorado Street
Stockton, California 95202-1997
Telephone (209) 944-8333
Telefax (209) 944-8898

DEPUTIES
Barbara J. Anderson    Michael T. Rishwain
Cynthia L. Humbert     Lauren P. Stout
Guy D. Petzold         Bart J. Thiltgen
Mark E. Tribble

INVESTIGATOR
Steven J. Hunt

R. Thomas Harris
City Attorney
John T. Moran
Assistant City Attorney

March 29, 1991

John Wallace, Esq.
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE

On March 29, 1991, culminating several prior conversations, the following facts were presented at the request of Stockton City Councilmember Mel Panizza for the purpose of ascertaining the existence of a potential conflict of interest.

The Councilmember owns a landscaping and gardening business which is a user of one of the City's landfills. The Council and a Council Committee will consider increasing the tipping fees for use of the landfill. The use of this facility is limited to City operations from garden refuse pickup and to certain local landscaping and gardening interests for deposit of garden refuse generated within the City. Tipping fees are paid by landscapers and gardeners. The question to be considered by the Committee and the Council is whether the fees for use of the facility should be increased.

This is to confirm my understanding of the analysis and opinion as stated today that the Councilmember may take part in the deliberations and vote on the matter. The decision by the Council will have a financial effect by increasing costs to the Councilmember if increased fees are adopted or by cost avoidance if the decision is not to act or not increase the fees.

As a business owner, the Councilmember would be impacted since the business is a source of income exceeding $250.00 during the prior year. This impact appears to be reasonably foreseeable. Where the Council's decision indirectly involves the financial interest, the financial effect on the small business is not material under Section 18702.2(g) of the Regulations unless the $2,500 dollar impact on expenses is met. It is anticipated that even a doubling of the fees

Mr. John Wallace  
March 29, 1991

will not meet that threshold amount. In addition, if the threshold for the Councilmember's business is not met, it is unlikely that any client, as a source of income to the Councilmember, will be impacted materially regardless of the section applicable. Therefore, the Councilmember may, based on the above facts, participate in the decision at the Committee and Council levels.

Your assistance and cooperation in dealing with this matter are most appreciated. It is my understanding that the Committee may consider the matter within the next two weeks.

Please confirm my understanding of your opinion and advice at the earliest possible time.

R. THOMAS HARRIS  
CITY ATTORNEY

By [signature]  
BARBARA J. ANDERSON  
DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY

BJA/dmg
